They work with tools like multimeters, cable crimpers, power drills, and oscilloscopes, reading wiring diagrams and schematics to fault-find and repair machinery or PLC-controlled systems. While some digital elements exist — such as programming or adjusting frequency converters and basic PLC parameters — the core of the job is physical: pulling cables, terminating connections, and ensuring machinery runs safely and reliably.

name: Fin-, inrednings- och möbelsnickare
rationale: Cabinet-making is a fundamentally physical and craft-based occupation requiring manual dexterity, tactile judgment, and hands-on woodworking that AI cannot directly automate. Some indirect exposure exists through CNC programming and CAD-assisted design, but the core work of shaping, assembling, and finishing wood products demands physical presence and skilled craftsmanship that provides a strong barrier to AI displacement.
description: En fin-, inrednings- och möbelsnickare tillverkar, monterar och reparerar möbler, inredningsdetaljer och finsnickeri i trä, exempelvis kök, skåp, trappor och lister. Arbetet är utpräglat fysiskt och hantverksmässigt – de använder handverktyg som stämjärn, hyvlar och sågar samt maskiner som fräsar, svarvar, bandsågar och CNC-maskiner. Visst digitalt inslag förekommer, framför allt vid programmering av CNC-utrustning eller läsning av ritningar i CAD, men kärnan i arbetet är praktisk bearbetning av trä och liknande material.

name: Skräddare och ateljésömmerskor m.fl.
rationale: Tailors and seamstresses perform highly physical, hands-on craft work involving cutting, sewing, fitting, and shaping fabric to individual customer measurements, creating strong barriers to direct AI automation. While CAD-based pattern design tools introduce some digital exposure, the core work requires manual dexterity, tactile judgment, and in-person fittings that AI cannot replicate in the near term.
description: Skräddare och ateljésömmerskor arbetar huvudsakligen med att tillverka, anpassa och reparera kläder och andra textilprodukter, ofta efter individuella kundmått och önskemål. De använder fysiska verktyg som symaskiner, handsömnadsnålar, saxar, måttband, strykjärn och mönster för att skära, sy och forma tyg. Arbetet är till övervägande del praktiskt och hantverksmässigt, även om digitala hjälpmedel som CAD-baserad mönsterkonstruktion förekommer i vissa ateljéer.

name: Provsmakare och kvalitetsbedömare
name_en: Food and beverage tasters and graders
rationale: Food and beverage tasting fundamentally requires physical human senses — smell, taste, and texture perception — which AI cannot replicate, creating a strong natural barrier to automation. While AI and sensors can assist with some objective measurements (pH, refractometry, spectroscopy), the core sensory evaluation and qualitative judgment still depends on human perception, though AI tools may reduce the number of tasters needed over time by handling more routine quality checks.
description: En provsmakare och kvalitetsbedömare inom mat och dryck utför sensoriska bedömningar av livsmedel och drycker genom att systematiskt lukta, smaka och granska produkter för att säkerställa att de uppfyller fastställda kvalitetsstandarder. Arbetet är övervägande fysiskt och hands-on, och innefattar användning av standardiserade provningsprotokoll, mätinstrument som refraktometrar eller pH-mätare, samt sensoriska analysmetoder för att dokumentera smak, doft, textur och utseende. Resultaten registreras ofta i digitala system eller på pappersblanketter, men själva kärnan i arbetet är den direkta, fysiska kontakten med produkterna snarare än digitalt arbete.

name: Övriga livsmedelsförädlare
rationale: Food processing work is fundamentally physical and manual, requiring hands-on operation of machinery, handling of raw ingredients, and presence on a factory floor — all of which create strong barriers to AI automation. While AI and robotics can gradually automate some repetitive food processing tasks, full automation remains costly and technically challenging, especially for varied or non-standardized products, keeping overall AI exposure relatively low.
description: **Övriga livsmedelsförädlare (SSYK 7619)** utför praktiskt, fysiskt arbete med att bearbeta och förädla livsmedel som inte faller under mer specialiserade kategorier – till exempel beredning av kryddor, snacks, färdigmat eller andra matprodukter. De arbetar med maskiner, skärutrustning, blandare, värmebehandlingsutrustning och förpackningsmaskiner i en industri- eller produktionsmiljö. Arbetet är i huvudsak fysiskt och manuellt, även om enklare maskinövervakning och inmatning av parametrar i styrsystem kan förekomma.

name: Maskinoperatörer, cement-, sten- och betongvaror
name_en: Machine operator, cement, stone and other mineral products
rationale: This occupation is primarily physical, requiring hands-on operation of heavy industrial machinery, handling of raw materials like cement and aggregate, and quality checks in a factory environment — all strong barriers to AI automation. While AI/automation could optimize process control and monitoring systems over time, the physical manipulation, machinery operation, and real-time adjustments in a manufacturing setting significantly limit direct AI displacement.
description:
## Maskinoperatör – cement-, sten- och betongvaror (SSYK 8114)

En maskinoperatör inom detta område sköter och övervakar industriella maskiner och produktionslinjer som tillverkar produkter som betongblock, cementrör, takpannor eller stenprodukter – arbetet är övervägande **fysiskt** och utförs i fabriksmiljö. De hanterar råmaterial som cement, ballast och vatten, justerar maskininställningar, övervakar gjutnings- och pressningsprocesser samt kontrollerar att färdiga produkter håller rätt mått och kvalitet. Verktygen och utrustningen inkluderar formpressar, vibrationsmaskiner, blandningsaggregat och transportband, och även om enklare digitala kontroll

name: Övriga maskin- och processoperatörer vid stål- och metallverk
name_en: Metal processing plant operators not elsewhere classified
rationale: Metal processing plant operators work in industrial facilities requiring direct physical interaction with heavy machinery, molten or solid metals, and production equipment, creating strong barriers to AI automation. While AI and automation can optimize process control and monitoring via HMI systems, the hands-on adjustment, quality inspection with physical instruments, maintenance, and real-time troubleshooting in a hazardous environment means human presence remains essential for the foreseeable future.
description:
## Övriga maskin- och processoperatörer vid stål- och metallverk (SSYK 8129)

Dessa operatörer sköter och övervakar maskiner och processutrustning vid stål- och metallverk som inte faller under mer specifika kategorier, till exempel utrustning för ytbehandling, valsning, dragning eller annan metallbearbetning. Arbetet är övervägande **fysiskt** och innebär att starta, justera och övervaka maskiner, kontrollera metallens kvalitet och dimensioner med mätverktyg som skjutmått och mikrometer, samt utföra enklare underhåll och felsökning. Digitala inslag förekommer i form av manöverpaneler, HMI-skärmar (Human-Machine Interface)

name: Maskinoperatörer, gummiindustri
rationale: Rubber machine operators work on physical factory floors, handling raw materials, operating heavy industrial equipment, and performing tactile quality inspections that require hands-on presence and manual adjustment. While some automation and robotics could gradually reduce headcount over time, the physical nature of the work, need for real-time sensory judgment, and factory-floor setting provide significant barriers to AI-driven displacement in the near term.
description:
## Maskinoperatörer, gummiindustri (SSYK 8141)

These workers operate and monitor industrial machinery used to produce rubber products, such as mixing mills, extruders, vulcanization presses, and molding equipment. Their daily work involves setting up machines, adjusting parameters like temperature, pressure, and cycle times, feeding raw rubber compounds into equipment, and inspecting finished products for defects and dimensional accuracy. The work is primarily **physical and hands-on**, taking place on a factory floor, though some modern equipment involves digital control panels (HMI/PLC systems) for monitoring and adjustment.

name: Maskinoperatörer, plastindustri
rationale: This occupation is fundamentally physical and floor-based, requiring manual loading of materials, hands-on machine setup, and in-person quality inspection in an industrial environment — strong barriers to direct AI automation. While AI and automation can optimize machine parameters, monitor quality via sensors, and reduce the number of operators needed over time, the physical presence and manual dexterity required keep AI exposure relatively low, similar to other skilled trades.
description:
## Maskinoperatörer, plastindustri (SSYK 8142)

These workers set up, operate, and monitor machines used to produce plastic products, such as injection moulding machines, extruders, or blow moulding equipment. Their daily work is **primarily physical and hands-on** – they load raw plastic materials (granules or pellets), adjust machine settings like temperature, pressure, and cycle times, perform visual quality checks on finished parts, and carry out basic maintenance and troubleshooting. While modern machines may have digital control panels (HMI/PLC interfaces) that operators interact with, the core of the work remains manual and floor-based in an industrial production environment.

name: Maskinoperatörer, pappersvaruindustri
rationale: Machine operators in the paper products industry work on physical factory floors, manually setting up machinery, feeding materials, and performing hands-on quality checks and maintenance — tasks that require physical presence and dexterity that AI cannot currently automate. While AI and automation can optimize production monitoring and scheduling, the core physical manipulation and real-time mechanical troubleshooting on the production line provide a significant barrier to full AI displacement.
description:
**Maskinoperatörer, pappersvaruindustri (SSYK 8143)**

These workers operate and monitor industrial machines that convert paper and cardboard into finished products such as packaging, boxes, bags, or hygiene products. Their daily work involves setting up and adjusting machinery, feeding raw materials, checking product quality, and performing basic maintenance and troubleshooting on the production line. The work is primarily **physical and hands-on**, taking place on a factory floor, though modern equipment often includes digital control panels (HMI/PLC systems) for monitoring machine parameters and production data.

name: Maskinoperatörer, blekning, färgning och tvättning
name_en: Machine operators, bleaching, dyeing and cleaning
rationale: This occupation is fundamentally physical and hands-on, requiring workers to load/unload machinery, handle chemicals, and operate industrial equipment in a factory setting — tasks that cannot be performed remotely or automated by AI software alone. While AI could optimize process parameters or quality inspection to some degree, the core manual labor and physical presence requirements create a strong barrier to AI-driven displacement.
description:
## Maskinoperatörer, blekning, färgning och tvättning (SSYK 8151)

These workers operate and monitor industrial machines used to bleach, dye, or wash textile materials, yarn, or finished garments in factories or laundry facilities. Their daily work is highly **physical and hands-on**, involving loading and unloading machines, mixing and measuring chemicals or dye solutions, setting machine parameters (temperature, time, speed), and inspecting finished products for quality. They use equipment such as industrial dyeing vats, bleaching machines, centrifuges, and washing drums, and may handle basic digital control panels to program machine cycles, though the core of the work remains manual and process-oriented.

name: Övriga maskinoperatörer, textil-, skinn- och läderindustri
name_en: Machine operators not elsewhere classified, textile, fur and leather products
rationale: These machine operators work in physical industrial environments, manually feeding materials, monitoring specialized textile/leather machinery, and performing hands-on quality control and minor repairs — tasks that inherently require physical presence and dexterity. While some digital control panels are involved, AI cannot directly replace the physical manipulation and real-time mechanical troubleshooting required, though automation and robotics could gradually reduce headcount over time.
description:
## Övriga maskinoperatörer, textil-, skinn- och läderindustri (SSYK 8159)

Dessa operatörer sköter och övervakar specialiserade industrimaskiner inom textil-, skinn- och läderproduktion som inte faller under mer specifika yrkeskategorier – exempelvis maskiner för stoppning, filttillverkning, snörtillverkning eller ytbehandling av läder. Arbetet är till övervägande del **fysiskt och manuellt**, där de ställer in, startar och övervakar maskiner, matar in material, kontrollerar kvalitet på löpande band och åtgärdar enklare driftstopp. Digitala inslag förekommer i form av styrpaneler och ibland datoriserade styrsystem (C

name: Maskinoperatörer, kött- och fiskberedningsindustri
rationale: This role requires physical presence in cold, wet industrial environments operating and adjusting heavy machinery for meat and fish processing, creating strong barriers to AI automation. While HMI panels and monitoring systems introduce some digital exposure, the hands-on machine setup, sensory quality checks, and real-time physical adjustments keep AI displacement potential low in the near term.
description: **Maskinoperatörer inom kött- och fiskberedningsindustri** hanterar och övervakar industriella maskiner för styckning, malning, rökning, saltning, filering och förpackning av kött- och fiskprodukter. Arbetet är utpräglat fysiskt och utförs i kylda eller fuktiga produktionslokaler, där operatören ställer in, startar och justerar maskiner som fyllningsmaskiner, skivningsmaskiner och vakuumförpackningsmaskiner, samt utför enklare underhåll och felsökning. Digitala inslag förekommer i form av styrpaneler och HMI-skärmar (Human Machine Interface) för att övervaka produktionsparametrar, men kärnarbetet är i hög grad

name: Maskinoperatörer, mejeri
rationale: Dairy machine operators work in physical industrial environments requiring hands-on machine operation, maintenance, sample collection, and real-time process adjustments that AI cannot easily replicate remotely. While modern dairies use SCADA/HMI systems that AI could increasingly monitor and optimize, the physical presence, manual interventions, and sensory quality checks create strong barriers to full automation, keeping AI exposure relatively low.
description:
## Maskinoperatörer, mejeri (SSYK 8162)

Maskinoperatörer inom mejeri sköter och övervakar industriella maskiner och processutrustning för bearbetning av mjölk och tillverkning av mejeriprodukter som ost, smör, yoghurt och mjölk – detta inkluderar pastörisering, separering, homogenisering och förpackning. Arbetet är primärt fysiskt och processorienterat, där operatören startar, justerar och kontrollerar maskiner, utför enklare underhåll samt tar prover för kvalitetskontroll. Moderna mejerier använder ofta datoriserade styrsystem (HMI/SCADA) vilket innebär ett visst inslag av digital övervakning via kontrollpaneler, men

name: Maskinoperatörer, kvarn-, bageri- och konfektyrindustri
name_en: Machine operators, baked-goods, cereal and chocolate-products
rationale: This occupation is fundamentally physical and floor-based, requiring hands-on operation of industrial machinery, manual handling of materials, and on-site troubleshooting — strong barriers to direct AI automation. While AI and automation may incrementally optimize production parameters or reduce staffing needs over time, the physical nature of the work and the need to be present on the production floor limits AI exposure significantly.
description:
## Maskinoperatörer, kvarn-, bageri- och konfektyrindustri (SSYK 8163)

These workers operate and monitor industrial production machines used to process grain, bake bread, or manufacture confectionery and chocolate products – tasks include feeding raw materials into equipment, adjusting settings for temperature, speed, and mixing ratios, and ensuring consistent product quality. The work is **primarily physical and hands-on**, involving direct interaction with large mechanical equipment such as industrial mixers, ovens, conveyor belts, extruders, and packaging machines. While some modern facilities incorporate digital control panels (HMI/SCADA systems) for monitoring production parameters, the core of the job remains manual – cleaning machinery, troubleshooting mechanical issues, and handling raw ingredients and finished goods on the production floor.

name: Operatörer inom sågverk, hyvleri och plywood m.m.
name_en: Wood processing, sawmill and plywood plant operators
rationale: Sawmill and plywood plant operators work in physical, industrial environments handling heavy timber and machinery, which presents a strong barrier to AI automation. While AI and automation can optimize cutting patterns, monitor quality, and control some machine parameters, the hands-on physical tasks, real-time adjustments to irregular natural materials, and factory floor presence keep overall AI exposure low.
description:
## Operatörer inom sågverk, hyvleri och plywood (SSYK 8173)

These workers operate and monitor industrial machinery in sawmills, planing mills, and plywood plants — tasks include feeding timber into saws, adjusting cutting equipment, and overseeing the processing of logs into boards, veneers, or panels. They work with physical tools and heavy industrial machines such as band saws, frame saws, planers, and hydraulic log handlers, and may use control panels or basic PLC/HMI interfaces to set machine parameters and monitor production flow. The work is predominantly **physical and hands-on**, taking place on the factory floor in noisy, dusty environments, though digital monitoring systems play an increasing supporting role.

name: Maskinoperatörer inom ytbehandling, trä
rationale: This occupation is firmly rooted in physical factory-floor work — feeding materials, adjusting mechanical equipment, monitoring spray nozzles, and hands-on quality checks — creating strong barriers to direct AI automation. While AI and automation could incrementally improve machine control and quality monitoring (e.g., computer vision for coating consistency), the job fundamentally requires physical presence and manual intervention, keeping AI exposure low.
description:
## Maskinoperatörer inom ytbehandling, trä (SSYK 8174)

These workers operate and monitor industrial machines used for surface treatment of wood and wood-based products, such as lacquering, painting, staining, varnishing, or impregnation lines. Their daily work is highly **physical and hands-on**, involving tasks like feeding workpieces into machines, adjusting settings for coating thickness and speed, monitoring spray nozzles or roller applicators, and ensuring consistent quality throughout production runs. They work with equipment such as spray booths, roller coating machines, impregnation tanks, and drying/UV-curing ovens, and may perform basic maintenance and troubleshoot minor faults – with some digital interaction through machine control panels or PLC interfaces, but the core of the job remains manual and machine-oriented on the factory floor.

name: Andra process- och maskinoperatörer
name_en: Machine operators not elsewhere classified, stationary plant and
rationale: These machine operators work in physical industrial environments, monitoring and adjusting stationary equipment, feeding materials, and performing hands-on maintenance — tasks that require physical presence and manual dexterity that AI cannot directly automate. While AI and automation can optimize process monitoring and reduce headcount over time through smarter control systems, the physical nature of the role and need for on-site intervention provide a meaningful barrier to full AI exposure.
description:
## Andra process- och maskinoperatörer (SSYK 8189)

These workers operate and monitor stationary industrial machines and process equipment that don't fall under more specific operator categories, such as specialized mixing, crushing, filtering, or packaging machinery. Their daily work is primarily **physical and hands-on**, involving starting, adjusting, and supervising equipment, performing basic maintenance, and ensuring raw materials are fed correctly into production processes. They use control panels, manual instruments, and increasingly simple digital monitoring systems, but the core of their work remains rooted in the physical production environment rather than desk-based or purely digital tasks.

name: Lokförare
rationale: Train operators require physical presence in the cab, manual control of locomotive systems, and real-time situational awareness of tracks, signals, and passengers, creating strong barriers to direct AI automation. While autonomous train technology exists and is advancing (e.g., driverless metros), mainline freight and passenger rail still broadly requires human operators for safety, regulatory, and infrastructure reasons, though AI-assisted driving aids (ERTMS/ATC) are gradually reducing cognitive demands and may reduce headcounts over time.
description: En lokförare framför tåg – både persontåg och godståg – enligt fastställda tidtabeller och säkerhetsrutiner. Det dagliga arbetet innebär att hantera lokets/tågets reglage och kontrollpanel (bromsar, acceleration, signalsystem), kommunicera via radio med trafikledningen samt övervaka spår, signaler och instrumentering längs hela sträckan. Arbetet är primärt fysiskt-operativt i kombination med digitala hjälpmedel som datorbaserade trafikinformationssystem (exempelvis ERTMS/ATC) och elektroniska körorder.

name: Övriga bil-, motorcykel- och cykelförare
name_en: Motorcycle, bicycle drivers and other automobile drivers not elsewhere classified
rationale: This occupation is fundamentally physical and mobile — requiring a human to physically operate a vehicle and deliver items in the real world, which AI cannot directly automate in the near term. While AI optimizes routing and dispatch algorithms, the core task of physically driving and delivering remains human-dependent, though autonomous delivery vehicles and drones pose a longer-term displacement risk.
description:
## Övriga bil-, motorcykel- och cykelförare (SSYK 8329)

These workers primarily perform **physical, hands-on transport and delivery work**, driving motorcycles, bicycles (including cargo bikes), mopeds, or various light vehicles to transport goods, documents, food, or passengers between locations. Their daily tasks include picking up and dropping off packages or deliveries, navigating urban or rural routes efficiently, and confirming deliveries — often using a **smartphone or handheld scanner** to log completed jobs, receive new assignments via dispatch apps (such as those used by courier or food delivery platforms), and obtain customer signatures. The work is overwhelmingly **physical and mobile** rather than desk-based, requiring good knowledge of local geography, traffic regulations, and basic vehicle maintenance awareness.

name: Buss- och spårvagnsförare
rationale: Bus and tram driving requires direct physical presence, manual vehicle operation, real-time situational awareness, and passenger interaction — strong barriers to AI automation in the near term. While autonomous vehicle technology is advancing and could eventually disrupt this role, full deployment faces significant regulatory, infrastructure, and safety hurdles, keeping current AI exposure relatively low but notably higher than purely manual trades.
description: En buss- och spårvagnsförare kör passagerare längs fastställda rutter enligt tidtabell, hanterar biljettförsäljning och kontrollerar färdbiljetter via digitala biljettsystem och kortläsare i fordonet. Arbetet är till stor del fysiskt och praktiskt – föraren manövrerar fordonet, övervakar trafiken och ansvarar för passagerarnas säkerhet, samtidigt som de kommunicerar med trafikledningen via radio eller digitala informationssystem. En viktig del av vardagen är också att informera resenärer om hållplatser och störningar samt att rapportera avvikelser och tekniska fel.

name: Lastbilsförare m.fl.
rationale: Heavy truck driving is fundamentally a physical, hands-on occupation requiring real-world presence, manual skill in vehicle operation, load securing, and navigating diverse road conditions — strong barriers to direct AI automation. However, autonomous vehicle technology poses a longer-term displacement risk, and AI already optimizes routing and logistics, justifying a score slightly above pure manual trades.
description: Swedish **lastbilsförare** kör och manövrerar tunga lastbilar och långtradare för att transportera gods, råvaror eller maskiner mellan terminaler, lager och kunder. Det dagliga arbetet är starkt fysiskt och praktiskt – det inkluderar lastsäkring, körning på väg och ibland hantering av truck eller bakgavellift vid av- och pålastning. De använder digitala verktyg som färdskrivare, GPS-navigation och transportadministrationssystem (TMS) för orderhantering och körjournaler, men kärnan i arbetet är fysisk och utförs primärt utanför kontoret.

name: Kranförare m.fl.
name_en: Crane, hoist and related plant operators
rationale: Crane and hoist operators work in physical environments requiring hands-on control of heavy machinery, real-time spatial judgment, and coordination with ground personnel — strong barriers to direct AI automation. While remote-controlled and semi-automated crane systems are advancing, full AI replacement remains limited by safety requirements, unpredictable site conditions, and the need for embodied situational awareness.
description:
## Kranförare m.fl. (SSYK 8343)

Kranförare manövrerar och opererar kranar, traverser, hissar och liknande lyftanordningar för att förflytta tunga material, gods eller konstruktionselement på byggarbetsplatser, i hamnar, på varv eller i industrimiljöer. Arbetet utförs från en förarhytt, en manöverpanel eller via radiostyrning, och kräver noggrann koordination med signalmän och övrig personal på marken för att säkerställa säker lasthantering. Arbetet är övervägande **fysiskt och operativt** till sin natur – det handlar om att hantera verkliga maskiner och gods i krävande miljöer – men inslag av digitala kontrollsystem och datorstöd

name: Truckförare
rationale: Forklift driving is a physically hands-on occupation requiring real-time spatial judgment, manual vehicle control, and presence in warehouses or production facilities, creating strong barriers to direct AI automation. While autonomous forklifts and AGVs (automated guided vehicles) are an emerging technology that could gradually reduce demand, widespread deployment remains limited and the occupation retains significant physical and situational complexity that keeps AI exposure relatively low for now.
description: A Swedish truckförare operates forklifts and other industrial trucks to move, load, and unload goods and materials in warehouses, terminals, or production facilities. Their work is highly physical and hands-on, involving driving forklifts (truck), pallet jacks, and reach trucks to stack pallets, transport goods, and supply production lines. Some digital elements exist, such as scanning barcodes with handheld terminals and registering movements in warehouse management systems (WMS), but the core of the work remains practical and physically active.

name: Handpaketerare och andra fabriksarbetare
rationale: This occupation involves repetitive physical tasks like packing, sorting, and labeling on production lines, which are targets for robotic automation rather than AI-driven software automation — the exposure comes primarily from physical robotics and conveyor automation, not AI per se. While industrial robots and automated packing systems are gradually displacing such roles, full automation remains constrained by cost, dexterity requirements, and varied product handling, keeping the near-term AI exposure moderate-to-low.
description:
## Handpaketerare och andra fabriksarbetare (SSYK 9320)

Dessa arbetare utför manuella, fysiska uppgifter i fabriker och produktionsanläggningar, såsom att packa, sortera, märka och transportera varor längs produktions- eller packningslinjer. De arbetar med enkla handverktyg, transportband, pallar, shrinkwrap-maskiner och förpackningsmaterial, och följer ofta ett repetitivt arbetsmönster styrt av produktionstakten. Arbetet är till övervägande del fysiskt och hands-on, med begränsad digital interaktion – möjligen skanning av streckkoder eller enkel registrering i ett produktionssystem.

name: Elevassistenter m.fl.
rationale: Elevassistenter provide predominantly hands-on, physical support to students with special needs — including mobility assistance, personal care, and real-time one-on-one human interaction — creating strong natural barriers to AI automation. While some digital tools may be used in the role, the core work is deeply interpersonal and physically present, placing it firmly in the low-exposure range similar to care assistants.
description: Swedish elevassistenter primarily provide hands-on, physical support to individual students with special needs or disabilities in classroom and school settings – helping them with practical tasks like mobility, personal care, eating, and navigating the school environment. They work closely alongside teachers, supporting students during lessons by clarifying instructions, providing one-on-one attention, and helping with learning materials such as worksheets, adapted texts, or assistive tools like communication boards or tablets. The work is predominantly physical and interpersonal rather than digital, though they may use some digital aids (such as speech-to-text software or learning apps) depending on the student's needs.

name: VVS-montörer m.fl.
rationale: Plumbing and heating installation is fundamentally a hands-on, physically demanding trade requiring manual dexterity, spatial problem-solving on-site, and the use of specialized tools in varied real-world environments that AI cannot automate directly. While AI may assist with planning, diagnostics, or documentation at the margins, the core work requires physical presence and skilled craftsmanship, placing it firmly in the low-exposure category similar to electricians and plumbers.
description: **VVS-montörer** installerar, underhåller och reparerar rör- och ledningssystem för vatten, värme, sanitet och ventilation i byggnader – exempelvis dragning av vattenledningar, montering av radiatorer, golvvärme och värmepumpar. Arbetet är utpräglat **fysiskt** och utförs på byggarbetsplatser eller hos kunder, där de använder handverktyg som rörtänger, rörbockar och svetsutrustning samt elkraft- och presstänger för kopplingar i koppar, plast och stål. Digitala inslag förekommer men är begränsade – främst ritningsläsning via surfplatta och enklare dokumentation – medan själva kärnarbetet handlar om manuellt hantverk och problem

name: Installations- och serviceelektriker
rationale: Electricians perform highly physical, hands-on work involving cable installation, panel mounting, and on-site troubleshooting that cannot be replicated remotely or automated by current AI systems. While AI may assist with diagnostics, scheduling, or reading digital schematics, the core manual labor requires physical presence, dexterity, and real-world problem-solving that provides a strong natural barrier to AI displacement.
description: En installations- och serviceelektriker arbetar främst fysiskt med att installera, underhålla och felsöka elektriska system i byggnader – exempelvis kabeldragning, montering av elcentraler, uttag, belysning och säkringssystem. De använder handverktyg som skruvmejslar, avbitartänger och kabelskalare tillsammans med mätinstrument som multimetrar och kabelprovare för att kontrollera spänning, ström och kontinuitet. Arbetet sker till övervägande del på plats ute hos kunder – på byggarbetsplatser, i fastigheter eller industrimiljöer – och är till sin natur utpräglat manuellt och fysiskt, även om digitala ritningar och felsökningsscheman i ökande utsträc

name: Hudterapeuter
rationale: Hudterapeuter perform almost entirely hands-on physical work directly on clients' skin using specialized equipment, creating a strong natural barrier to AI automation. While AI could assist marginally with skin condition assessment via imaging or optimize booking/treatment planning, the core value of the occupation lies in tactile manual procedures that cannot be replicated remotely or digitally.
description: Swedish hudterapeuter perform hands-on skin care treatments such as facials, chemical peels, microdermabrasion, and body treatments, working directly on clients' skin using professional equipment like steamers, high-frequency devices, ultrasound machines, and various cosmetic products. They assess clients' skin conditions, develop individualized treatment plans, and carry out procedures to address issues like acne, aging, pigmentation, or dehydration. The work is almost entirely physical and hands-on, taking place in treatment rooms at beauty salons, spas, or clinics, with minimal digital components beyond booking systems and possibly documenting client treatment histories.

name: Vårdare, boendestödjare
rationale: This role is fundamentally physical and interpersonal — providing hands-on personal care, emotional support, and daily life assistance to vulnerable individuals in their homes or residential facilities, which AI cannot replicate. The only meaningful AI exposure comes from the minor digital documentation component, where tools could assist with record-keeping, but this represents a small fraction of the workday.
description: A Vårdare/boendestödjare supports people with psychiatric disabilities, intellectual disabilities, or social difficulties in their daily lives, helping with tasks like personal hygiene, cooking, cleaning, medication management, and accompanying clients to appointments or activities. The work is predominantly **physical and interpersonal** – taking place in the client's home or a residential facility – and involves direct hands-on assistance and social/emotional support rather than computer-based work. Documentation of care measures is done digitally in care record systems (such as Treserva or Lifecare), but this constitutes only a small part of the workday.

name: Soldater m.fl.
rationale: Combat soldiers perform highly physical, field-based work requiring presence in complex, unpredictable environments — operating weapons, vehicles, and conducting patrols — which presents strong barriers to AI automation. While AI can assist with surveillance, logistics, and decision-support systems, the core tasks of infantry combat and physical military operations remain deeply resistant to full AI substitution.
description: Swedish "Soldater m.fl." (SSYK 0310) utför huvudsakligen fysiskt krävande arbete inom mark-, marin- eller flygstridskrafter, vilket inkluderar strid, bevakning, patrullering och hantering av militär utrustning som vapen, fordon, fartyg och tekniska system. De arbetar med en bred uppsättning verktyg och materiel – från handvapen och pansarfordon till kommunikationsradio och sensorsystem – och måste behärska både individuella och kollektiva stridstekniker. Arbetet är övervägande fysiskt och fältbaserat, men innefattar även viss digital hantering av kommunikations- och ledningssystem, kartor och teknisk utrustning.

name: Fritidspedagoger
rationale: Fritidspedagoger work is fundamentally physical, relational, and presence-dependent — supervising and engaging children aged 6–13 in hands-on activities like crafts, sports, and play in ways that cannot be replicated remotely or automated. The minor digital administrative component (documentation, parent communication) has some AI exposure, but it represents such a small fraction of the role that overall AI displacement potential remains very low.
description: **Fritidspedagoger** work primarily in school-age childcare (*fritidshem*) alongside children aged 6–13, planning and leading creative, social, and physical activities such as crafts, sports, drama, and free play during before- and after-school hours. Their work is overwhelmingly **hands-on and physical** – they spend most of their day actively engaging with children on the floor, outdoors, in gymnasiums, or in workshop spaces using materials like art supplies, sports equipment, and building tools. Administrative tasks such as documenting children's development and communicating with parents are done digitally (typically via platforms like Unikum or Skolplattformen), but this represents a small fraction of their workday compared to the direct, relational, and activity-based work with children.

name: Skolassistenter m.fl.
rationale: School assistants perform highly physical and interpersonal work including personal care, mobility assistance, and hands-on support for students with special needs, creating strong barriers to AI automation. While AI tools may marginally assist with some administrative or learning-support tasks, the core role demands constant physical presence and human empathy that AI cannot meaningfully replace.
description: **Swedish School Assistants (Skolassistenter, SSYK 4116)** primarily support teachers and students in the classroom by helping individual pupils – often those with special needs or disabilities – with practical tasks such as personal care, mobility assistance, and participation in lessons. Their work is largely **physical and interpersonal**, involving hands-on support like accompanying students between classrooms, assisting with materials, and helping maintain structure and routines during the school day. Digital tools play a minor role, though they may use tablets or assistive technology devices to support students' learning, alongside physical tools like adapted learning materials and mobility equipment.

name: Bartendrar
rationale: Bartending is fundamentally a physical, in-person occupation requiring manual skill with tools, real-time social interaction, and on-site presence — all strong barriers to direct AI automation. AI may assist marginally with inventory management or POS systems, but the core work of mixing drinks, reading guest moods, and maintaining a lively bar environment cannot be performed remotely or replaced by AI in any near-term scenario.
description: Swedish bartenders (**Bartendrar**, SSYK 5132) blandar och serverar alkoholhaltiga och alkoholfria drycker vid en bardisk, tar emot beställningar från gäster och hanterar betalningar. Det dagliga arbetet är utpräglat **fysiskt** och inkluderar användning av verktyg som shakers, jiggers, strainers, isplockar, sodamaskiner och kaffemaskiner, samt kassasystem (POS-terminaler) för orderhantering. De ansvarar också för att hålla bardisken ren och välorganiserad, fylla på förråd och säkerställa att gäster inte serveras alkohol i strid med Alkohollagen.

name: Övriga skönhets- och kroppsterapeuter
name_en: Beauty and body therapists professionals not elsewhere classified
rationale: This occupation is almost entirely hands-on and physical, involving direct touch-based treatments like massage, wraps, and skincare that cannot be performed remotely or automated by AI in any meaningful way. Minor administrative tasks like appointment booking may be AI-assisted, but the core work requires physical presence and manual skill, placing it firmly in the low-exposure category alongside other care and manual service workers.
description: Swedish **övriga skönhets- och kroppsterapeuter** (SSYK 5149) perform hands-on physical treatments such as various massage therapies, body wraps, skincare treatments, waxing, and wellness therapies that don't fall under more specialized categories like traditional massage or cosmetology. They work directly with clients using physical tools and products such as massage tables, hot stones, wrapping materials, oils, and various skincare or therapeutic equipment. The work is almost entirely physical and client-facing, typically carried out in spas, wellness centers, or beauty salons, with only minor administrative or digital tasks such as booking appointments or managing client records.

name: Fastighetsskötare
rationale: Building caretakers perform predominantly physical, hands-on work — minor repairs, snow removal, cleaning, and HVAC management — that requires direct on-site presence and manual dexterity, creating a strong natural barrier to AI automation. While digital tools like BMS platforms and work-order apps assist with administration and fault reporting, these represent a minor portion of the role and AI's impact here is limited to modest productivity gains in scheduling and fault detection rather than displacement.
description: A Swedish Fastighetsskötare performs primarily **physical, hands-on work** maintaining residential and commercial properties — this includes minor repairs (fixing locks, doors, pipes, and electrical fittings), cleaning common areas, managing heating and ventilation systems, and handling snow removal and outdoor maintenance. They use hand tools, power tools, and building management systems (BMS/fastighetsautomation) to monitor heating, water, and energy systems, and may use mobile apps or tablets for logging work orders and communicating with property managers. The role is overwhelmingly field-based rather than desk-based, with digital tools serving mainly as a support function for administration and fault reporting.

name: Begravnings- och krematoriepersonal
rationale: This occupation is fundamentally physical and manual — handling, embalming, and preparing human remains, performing cremations, and placing bodies in caskets — tasks that require direct physical presence and cannot be automated by AI. Some minor administrative tasks like documentation and booking could see AI assistance, but the core work remains highly resistant to AI displacement.
description: Swedish begravnings- och krematoriepersonal hanterar kroppar av avlidna genom att utföra balsamering, smink och iordningställande inför visning, samt sköter kremationer och kistläggning. Arbetet är övervägande fysiskt och manuellt, med verktyg som balsamerings- och kremationsutrustning, lyftanordningar och omvårdnadsmaterial, men innefattar även viss administrativ hantering av dokumentation och bokningar. De samarbetar nära med anhöriga och begravningsbyråer för att säkerställa ett värdigt omhändertagande enligt önskemål och lagkrav.

name: Barnsköterskor
rationale: Assistant nurses working with children perform highly physical, hands-on caregiving tasks — diapering, feeding, comforting, and facilitating play — that fundamentally require physical presence and direct human interaction with young children. While some documentation is digitized, AI cannot replicate the tactile care, emotional attunement, and real-time physical responsiveness that define this role, placing it firmly in the low-exposure range alongside other care-oriented physical occupations.
description: **Barnsköterskor** arbetar med omvårdnad och omsorg om barn, framför allt på förskolor, barnavdelningar eller BVC, och deras arbete är övervägande **fysiskt och hands-on** – de byter blöjor, hjälper barn med mat och påklädning, tröstar och stimulerar barns utveckling genom lek och rutiner. De använder enkla praktiska hjälpmedel som omvårdnadsutrustning, leksaker och pedagogiskt material snarare än avancerade digitala verktyg, även om viss dokumentation (t.ex. journalföring eller dagliga anteckningar) kan ske i digitala system som Treserva eller liknande. Samarbete med föräldrar, sjuksköterskor och pedagoger är en central

name: Vårdbiträden
name_en: Home-based personal care and related workers
rationale: Vårdbiträden perform deeply physical, hands-on personal care tasks — bathing, dressing, toileting, and mobility assistance — that require direct bodily presence and cannot be performed or meaningfully automated by AI. While digital documentation tools may modestly increase efficiency, the core work is irreducibly physical and interpersonal, placing it firmly in the low-exposure range alongside other hands-on care roles.
description: A Swedish vårdbiträde primarily performs **hands-on physical care tasks** in the home environment or residential care facilities, including helping clients with personal hygiene (washing, dressing, toileting), preparing meals, light household cleaning, and assisting with mobility. They use basic physical tools such as lifting aids (lyftsele, lift/hiss), hygiene products, and sometimes simple medical equipment like blood pressure monitors or medication dosettes. Documentation of carried-out measures is increasingly done digitally via mobile devices or tablets using care management systems (e.g., Treserva or TES), but the core of the work remains strongly **physical and interpersonal** rather than digital.

name: Brandmän
rationale: Firefighting is predominantly physical, requiring real-time emergency response, operation of heavy equipment, and hands-on rescue work that AI cannot replicate. Minor administrative tasks like logging and reporting have some AI exposure, but the core duties present a strong natural barrier to automation or AI-driven productivity displacement.
description: Swedish **Brandmän** primarily perform highly physical work responding to fires, traffic accidents, chemical spills, and rescue operations. They operate heavy equipment such as fire trucks, hydraulic rescue tools (like the "Jaws of Life"), hoses, breathing apparatus, and ladders to extinguish fires and extract injured persons. Between emergency calls, they conduct equipment checks, physical training, and fire prevention inspections, with only a minor administrative component involving digital tools for logging and reporting.

name: Kriminalvårdare
rationale: Prison guards require constant physical presence, hands-on security enforcement, direct human interaction, and real-time situational judgment in a high-stakes environment — all of which create strong barriers to AI automation. While digital tools and AI-assisted surveillance can support documentation and monitoring tasks, the core duties of physical supervision, restraint, cell searches, and inmate rehabilitation conversations remain fundamentally human-dependent.
description: A Swedish Kriminalvårdare works primarily in a **physical environment**, supervising and monitoring inmates in prisons or remand facilities, conducting cell checks, searches, and escorting prisoners within and outside the institution. They handle physical tools such as keys, locking systems, restraints, and surveillance equipment, while also using digital systems to document incidents, register inmate movements, and log daily activities. A significant part of the role also involves structured conversations and rehabilitation support with inmates, meaning the work combines security enforcement with social and motivational tasks.

name: Odlare av jordbruksväxter, frukt- och bär
rationale: Field crop and vegetable growing is fundamentally physical, outdoor work involving soil preparation, planting, irrigation, and harvesting that cannot be done remotely or replaced by AI directly. While precision agriculture tools like GPS-guided machinery, drones, and farm management software are increasingly used for planning and optimization, the core manual labor and real-world presence requirements create a strong natural barrier to AI displacement.
description: En odlare av jordbruksväxter, frukt- och bär utför huvudsakligen fysiskt arbete med att så, plantera, sköta och skörda grödor som spannmål, grönsaker, frukt eller bär. Det dagliga arbetet innefattar markberedning, bevattning, gödsling, ogräsbekämpning och skörd med hjälp av traktorer, skördetröskor, planteringsmaskiner och handredskap. Även om digitala hjälpmedel som GPS-styrda maskiner, drönare och affärssystem används i ökande utsträckning för planering och dokumentation, är kärnan i arbetet påtagligt fysiskt och utomhusbaserat.

name: Övriga djuruppfödare och djurskötare
name_en: Animal breeders and related workers not elsewhere classified
rationale: Animal breeders and handlers work directly with physical animals in hands-on environments requiring manual labor, sensory judgment, and real-time physical interaction — tasks AI cannot automate remotely. While AI may assist marginally with health monitoring sensors or feeding automation, the core work remains deeply physical and resistant to AI displacement.
description: **Övriga djuruppfödare och djurskötare (SSYK 6129)** arbetar med skötsel, utfodring och övervakning av djur som inte faller under mer specialiserade kategorier, exempelvis pälsdjur, katter, hundar, ormar eller exotiska djurarter. Det dagliga arbetet är utpräglat fysiskt och innefattar rengöring av utrymmen, tillredning av foder, hälsokontroller och hantering av djuren direkt. Som verktyg används bland annat utfodringsautomater, rengöringsutrustning och enkla journalsystem – den digitala komponenten är begränsad och handlar främst om grundläggande registrering av djurhälsa och fodergiva.

name: Växtodlare och djuruppfödare, blandad drift
rationale: Mixed crop and animal farming is overwhelmingly physical, outdoor work involving machinery operation, manual labor, and direct animal care that cannot be performed remotely or automated away by AI in the near term. While digital tools like GPS-guided machinery and record-keeping systems add some AI exposure at the margins, the core tasks require physical presence and hands-on skill, keeping exposure very low.
description: En växtodlare och djuruppfödare med blandad drift sköter både jordbruksmark och djurhållning – de plöjer, sår och skördar grödor med traktorer och lantbruksmaskiner, samtidigt som de utfodrar, övervakar och sköter djurens hälsa och välmående. Det dagliga arbetet är till övervägande del fysiskt och utomhusbaserat, med hantering av maskiner, redskap, foder och djur som centrala inslag. Digitala verktyg förekommer i ökande grad – exempelvis GPS-styrda maskiner, journalföringssystem för djur och växtskyddsregister – men utgör ett stöd snarare än kärnan i arbetet.

name: Träarbetare, snickare m.fl.
rationale: Carpentry and woodworking are fundamentally physical, hands-on trades requiring manual skill, spatial judgment, and on-site presence that AI cannot directly automate. While AI may marginally assist with design planning or CAD tooling, the core work of cutting, fitting, and assembling wood structures remains resistant to AI displacement.
description: **Träarbetare och snickare** utför fysiskt hantverk där de tillverkar, monterar och reparerar konstruktioner och produkter i trä – exempelvis husbyggnation, inredningssnickerier, fönster, dörrar och möbler. De arbetar med handverktyg som sågar, hammare och stämjärn samt eldrivna maskiner som cirkelsågar, sticksågar, borrmaskiner och hyvlar, och läser ritningar för att mäta, skära och montera material med precision. Arbetet är till övervägande del **fysiskt** och utförs på byggarbetsplatser, i verkstäder eller hos kunder, med begränsad digital inblandning – även om digitala ritverktyg (CAD) och digitala arbetssedlar fö

name: Lackerare och industrimålare
rationale: Industrial painting and lacquering is almost entirely physical and hands-on work requiring manual dexterity, spatial judgment, and on-site presence in workshops or factories, making direct AI automation very limited in the near term. While robotic painting systems exist in high-volume automotive manufacturing, most industrial painting contexts involve varied surfaces, complex geometries, and bespoke jobs that still require skilled human operators, keeping AI exposure low.
description: A lackerare och industrimålare performs hands-on physical work, applying paint, lacquer, varnish, and protective coatings to surfaces such as metal components, machinery, vehicles, and industrial structures. They use tools like spray guns, airbrushes, rollers, and brushes, along with mixing equipment to prepare the correct paint consistency and colour, and may operate sandblasting or grinding equipment to prepare surfaces beforehand. The work is entirely physical and hands-on, carried out in workshops, factories, or on-site, with little to no digital component beyond occasionally reading technical specifications or work orders.

name: Gjutare
rationale: Metal moulding is a highly physical, hands-on occupation performed in a hot, noisy foundry environment requiring manual dexterity with molten metal, heavy equipment, and physical inspection — creating strong barriers to direct AI automation. While AI may optimize mould designs, process parameters, or quality inspection through machine vision over time, the core physical tasks of preparing moulds, pouring metal, and finishing castings will remain largely human-operated for the foreseeable future.
description: A Swedish gjutare works physically with producing metal castings by preparing and assembling moulds, pouring molten metal into these moulds, and then finishing the solidified castings through grinding, cleaning and inspection. They use tools and equipment such as moulding boxes (formbackar), ladles, furnaces, grinding machines and measuring instruments to ensure the castings meet dimensional and quality requirements. The work is almost entirely hands-on and physically demanding, taking place in a foundry (gjuteri) environment with high temperatures, noise and heavy materials.

name: Byggnads- och ventilationsplåtslagare
name_en: Sheet metal workers, building and ventilation
rationale: Sheet metal workers perform highly physical, hands-on fabrication and installation tasks on construction sites that require manual dexterity, spatial reasoning in real environments, and use of specialized tools — all strong barriers to AI automation. While AI may assist marginally with design, planning, or blueprint reading, the core craft work cannot be meaningfully automated or performed remotely, placing this occupation near the low end of AI exposure.
description: A Swedish byggnads- och ventilationsplåtslagare fabricates, installs, and maintains sheet metal components for ventilation systems, roofing, and building envelopes, including ducts, air handling units, gutters, and facade cladding. The work is highly physical and hands-on, using tools such as shears, bending machines, folding tools, drills, riveting tools, and welding equipment to cut, shape, and assemble metal sheets on construction sites or in workshops. Digital tools play a minor supporting role, primarily for reading technical drawings or installation blueprints, but the core of the job is manual craftsmanship and physical installation work.

name: Distributionselektriker
rationale: This role is fundamentally defined by physical, outdoor fieldwork — climbing poles, pulling cables, operating bucket trucks, and troubleshooting live grid infrastructure in all weather conditions — which creates a strong natural barrier to AI automation. While digital tools like GIS documentation and AI-assisted fault diagnostics may slightly augment or streamline planning tasks, the core hands-on labor cannot be performed remotely or replaced by AI in any near-term scenario.
description: A Distributionselektriker works primarily with **physical, outdoor fieldwork** – installing, maintaining, and repairing electrical distribution networks including overhead power lines, underground cables, transformers, and switching stations at low and medium voltage levels. Daily tasks involve climbing poles or working from bucket trucks, pulling and connecting cables, replacing fuses and insulators, and troubleshooting faults in the grid using tools like cable locators, multimeters, crimping tools, and hydraulic lifting equipment. Digital tools play a supporting role – such as tablets for work orders and GIS-based grid documentation – but the core of the job is hands-on, physically demanding work outdoors in all weather conditions.

name: Slaktare och styckare m.fl.
name_en: Butchers, fishmongers and related food preparers
rationale: Butchers and meat/fish preparers perform highly physical, manual tasks requiring dexterity, sensory judgment, and cold-environment presence that AI cannot directly automate today. While robotic cutting and portioning technology exists in large industrial settings, the craft-based, variable nature of the work and minimal digital footprint keeps AI exposure low, comparable to other skilled trades.
description: Swedish **slaktare och styckare** work primarily with **physical, hands-on tasks** such as slaughtering animals, deboning and portioning carcasses, trimming fat and sinew, and preparing cuts of meat or fish for sale or further processing. They use tools like boning knives, band saws, cleavers, meat grinders, and cutting tables, often working in cold environments such as slaughterhouses, styckningshallar (cutting halls), or fish processing facilities. Digital elements are minimal and largely limited to basic registration of weights or labels, meaning the core of their work is entirely manual and craft-based.

name: Gruv- och stenbrottsarbetare
rationale: Mining and quarry work is highly physical, performed in harsh underground or open-pit environments requiring direct manual operation of heavy machinery, drilling, blasting, and material handling — all strong barriers to AI automation. While modern equipment increasingly uses digital control systems and autonomous vehicles are emerging in some large mines, the physically demanding, safety-critical, and variable-environment nature of the work keeps AI exposure relatively low.
description: **Gruv- och stenbrottsarbetare** utför fysiskt krävande arbete med att bryta berg, malm eller sten i underjordiska gruvor eller öppna dagbrott. De borrar hål i bergmassor, laddar och detonerar sprängmedel, hanterar tunga maskiner som borriggar, lastmaskiner och truckar, samt transporterar och sorterar det brutna materialet. Arbetet är till övervägande del praktiskt och fysiskt, och utförs i tuffa miljöer med buller, damm och sprängningar – även om moderna maskiner och utrustning i ökande grad styrs med digitala kontrollsystem och instrumentpaneler.

name: Övriga maskinoperatörer inom livsmedelsindustri m.m.
name_en: Machine operators not elsewhere classified, food and related products
rationale: These machine operators work predominantly on the physical factory floor, setting up, feeding, and monitoring industrial processing equipment in food and related industries — tasks requiring direct physical presence and manual intervention that AI cannot easily automate. While some modest efficiency gains may come from AI-assisted process monitoring or predictive maintenance, the core hands-on nature of the work and the relatively low-complexity digital interfaces provide strong barriers to significant AI displacement.
description:
## Övriga maskinoperatörer inom livsmedelsindustri m.m. (SSYK 8169)

These workers operate and monitor specialized processing machines in food, beverage, tobacco, or related industries that don't fall under more specific categories – for example equipment for spice blending, tea/coffee processing, salt refining, or animal feed production. Their daily work is predominantly **physical and hands-on**: they set up, adjust, feed materials into, and oversee machinery, perform basic maintenance, and check that products meet quality and hygiene standards. Tools include industrial processing equipment, conveyor systems, measuring instruments, and basic control panels, with some digital interaction through PLC-based machine interfaces, though advanced digital work is limited.

name: Montörer, träprodukter
rationale: This role is almost entirely hands-on physical assembly using tools, jigs, and machinery in a factory or workshop setting, creating a strong natural barrier to direct AI automation. While AI-driven robotics and automated assembly lines may gradually reduce the number of workers needed over time, the manual dexterity, spatial judgment, and physical presence required keep AI exposure low in the near term.
description: A Swedish **Montör inom träprodukter** primarily performs hands-on physical assembly of wood-based products such as furniture, windows, doors, or prefabricated building elements, using hand tools, power tools (drills, nail guns, sanders), and often jigs or assembly fixtures to join and fit components together accurately. The work involves reading technical drawings or assembly instructions, measuring and cutting materials, and operating machinery such as presses or automated assembly lines depending on the workplace. The role is almost entirely physical and manual in nature, with minimal digital interaction beyond occasionally reading digital work orders or instructions on a screen.

name: Anläggningsmaskinförare m.fl.
rationale: Heavy equipment operators work outdoors in physically demanding environments, manually controlling large machinery in unpredictable terrain — tasks that fundamentally cannot be automated by AI in the near term. While GPS-guided machine control and semi-autonomous features add some AI assistance, full remote or autonomous operation of excavators and bulldozers on complex, variable construction sites remains a distant prospect, keeping AI exposure low.
description: **Anläggningsmaskinförare** operates heavy construction machinery such as excavators, wheel loaders, bulldozers, and graders to perform earthmoving, grading, digging, and material handling on construction sites, road projects, and infrastructure works. Their daily work is highly **physical and hands-on**, involving maneuvering large machines in demanding outdoor environments, reading terrain, and precisely executing tasks like trenching, leveling ground, or loading materials. While modern machines increasingly include digital cab displays, GPS-guided grading systems, and machine control technology, the core work remains **physically demanding and machine-based** rather than office- or screen-oriented.

name: Kabinpersonal m.fl.
rationale: Cabin crew work is fundamentally physical and location-dependent, requiring in-person safety demonstrations, emergency response, food/beverage service, and direct passenger interaction aboard aircraft — none of which can be performed remotely or automated by AI in any near-term scenario. While AI may marginally optimize scheduling, passenger management systems, or digital manuals, the core job duties are strongly protected by their physical, regulatory, and human-presence requirements.
description: **Kabinpersonal (SSYK 5111)** arbetar ombord på flygplan och ansvarar för passagerarnas säkerhet och komfort under flygningar. Deras arbete är övervägande **fysiskt och serviceinriktat** – de utför säkerhetskontroller av kabinen, demonstrerar säkerhetsprocedurer, serverar mat och dryck, och hanterar nödsituationer enligt fastställda rutiner. De verktyg de använder är primärt fysiska (säkerhetsutrustning, servicetralleys, kommunikationssystem ombord), men digitala inslag förekommer i form av handdatorer för beställningar och passagerarhantering samt digitala manualer och rapporteringssystem.

name: Kockar och kallskänkor
rationale: Cooking and cold-buffet management is overwhelmingly physical and hands-on, requiring manual dexterity, sensory judgment, and real-time kitchen presence that AI cannot replicate. While AI may assist with menu planning, inventory management, or recipe suggestions, the core work of physically preparing and presenting food remains largely beyond automation.
description: Swedish cooks and cold-buffet managers (kallskänkor) prepare, cook and present food in restaurants, canteens, hotels and catering operations, working with tasks such as boiling, frying, baking, and assembling cold dishes and buffets. Their work is overwhelmingly physical and hands-on, using tools like knives, pots, pans, ovens, grills, and refrigeration equipment on a daily basis. Digital elements are minimal and typically limited to reading digital orders, using simple POS-linked ticket systems in the kitchen, or occasionally consulting digital recipes.

name: Övrig servicepersonal
name_en: Personal services workers not elsewhere classified
rationale: This catch-all category covers highly physical, hands-on service roles like pool attendants, crematorium workers, shoe shiners, and animal caretakers — all requiring physical presence and manual interaction with objects, environments, or living beings. AI has very limited ability to automate these tasks directly, though administrative and scheduling aspects may see minor AI assistance.
description: **Övrig servicepersonal (SSYK 5169)** utför praktiska serviceuppgifter som inte passar in under mer specifika yrkeskategorier, exempelvis arbete som badmästare, krematoriearbetare, skoputs­are eller djurpassare. Arbetet är till övervägande del **fysiskt** och utförs på plats – man hanterar konkreta objekt, miljöer eller levande varelser snarare än att arbeta vid en dator. Verktygen varierar kraftigt beroende på specialisering men kan inkludera rengöringsutrustning, handinstrument, djurvårdsutrustning eller teknisk utrustning för den specifika servicemiljön.

name: Barnskötare
rationale: Child care workers perform almost entirely physical, hands-on, and interpersonal tasks — supervising play, feeding, comforting, and nurturing young children — which cannot be automated or meaningfully augmented by AI. The minor digital documentation component (e.g., Unikum) represents a very small fraction of the role and poses negligible displacement risk.
description: A Barnskötare works primarily in preschools (förskolor) assisting in the daily care and education of young children, with hands-on tasks like organizing play activities, helping children with meals, nap routines, and outdoor activities. The work is almost entirely physical and interpersonal — they use everyday materials like arts and crafts supplies, toys, and outdoor equipment rather than digital tools, though some documentation of children's development may be logged in digital systems like Unikum or similar platforms. Their core role is to support the lead preschool teachers (förskollärare) while maintaining a safe, nurturing, and stimulating environment for the children.

name: Personliga assistenter
rationale: This role is almost entirely physical and interpersonal — assisting with hygiene, mobility, dressing, and daily activities requires hands-on human presence that AI cannot replicate in the foreseeable future. The only minor AI exposure comes from administrative tasks like documentation in care management systems, which represent a very small fraction of the work.
description: Swedish personliga assistenter provide hands-on physical support to individuals with functional disabilities, helping them with daily activities such as personal hygiene, dressing, eating, mobility, and participation in social or leisure activities. The work is almost entirely physical and interpersonal, carried out in the client's home or in community settings, with few digital tools beyond basic documentation in care management systems (such as Treserva or similar platforms) for logging hours and noting observations. The role is largely directed by the individual receiving assistance, who decides how and when support is provided according to their personal preferences and needs.

name: Trädgårdsodlare
rationale: Trädgårdsodlare work is overwhelmingly physical — planting, pruning, harvesting, and operating machinery — creating a strong natural barrier to direct AI automation. While AI and robotics are advancing in precision agriculture (sensors, automated irrigation, harvest robots), widespread deployment remains limited and the core hands-on labor still requires human presence, placing this occupation near the low end of AI exposure.
description: A Swedish Trädgårdsodlare performs highly physical work involving planting, cultivating, pruning, watering, fertilizing, and harvesting vegetables, fruits, flowers, or ornamental plants in market gardens, greenhouses, or nurseries. They work hands-on with tools such as spades, pruning shears, tractors, irrigation systems, and greenhouse equipment, and may operate machinery for soil preparation or harvesting. While some planning or inventory tasks may involve basic digital tools, the overwhelming majority of their daily work is practical and physical, carried out outdoors or in cultivation facilities.

name: Trädgårdsanläggare m.fl.
rationale: Gardeners and grounds workers perform physically demanding outdoor tasks — planting, mowing, pruning, and landscaping — that fundamentally require manual dexterity and physical presence, creating a strong natural barrier to AI automation. While AI could assist marginally with scheduling, route optimization, or design planning, the core hands-on work cannot be performed remotely or replaced by current AI systems.
description: **Trädgårdsanläggare** utför fysiskt krävande utomhusarbete med att anlägga, sköta och underhålla trädgårdar, parker och grönområden – detta inkluderar plantering, gräsklippning, beskärning, markarbeten och anläggning av gångar eller murar. De använder handverktyg som spadar, sekatörer och krattor tillsammans med maskinell utrustning som gräsklippare, grävmaskiner, trimmare och röjsågar. Arbetet är till övervägande del **fysiskt och utomhusbaserat**, med mycket begränsat digitalt inslag – möjligen enkel dokumentation eller kommunikation via mobil, men kärnuppgifterna är praktiska och manuella.

name: Uppfödare och skötare av sällskapsdjur
rationale: Pet breeding and care is a hands-on physical occupation requiring direct animal handling, cleaning, feeding, and health monitoring that cannot be automated by AI. While AI may assist marginally with record-keeping or customer communication, the vast majority of the work demands physical presence and manual interaction with animals, creating a strong barrier to AI displacement.
description: En uppfödare och skötare av sällskapsdjur arbetar huvudsakligen med att föda upp, mata, tvätta och sköta om sällskapsdjur som hundar, katter eller smådjur, samt sköta deras hälsa och välmående dagligen. Arbetet är utpräglat fysiskt och innefattar städning av burar och utrymmen, hantering av djurfoder och medicinsk grundvård, samt kontakt med djurägare och potentiella köpare. Digitala verktyg används i begränsad utsträckning, främst för journalföring, stamtavleregister och kundkommunikation, men merparten av arbetsdagen tillbringas i direktkontakt med djuren.

name: Skogsarbetare
rationale: Forestry work is overwhelmingly physical, conducted outdoors in demanding terrain and weather conditions, requiring manual operation of heavy machinery like harvesters and forwarders, chainsaw use, and hands-on planting and thinning — all tasks with very high barriers to AI automation. While GPS-guided harvester systems and autonomous forestry machines are emerging, full automation remains technologically and economically limited, and the irreducibly physical nature of the work keeps AI exposure very low.
description: A Swedish skogsarbetare performs physically demanding outdoor work including felling trees, delimbing, and clearing forest land, using tools such as chainsaws, harvester machines, and forwarders to process and transport timber. They plant seedlings, tend young forest stands, and carry out thinning operations to promote healthy forest growth. The work is overwhelmingly physical and conducted outdoors in all weather conditions, with limited digital components aside from basic machine controls and GPS-based work order systems used in modern harvester cabins.

name: Anläggningsdykare
rationale: Construction diving is almost entirely physical and manual work performed underwater — welding, cutting, bolting, and inspecting structures — requiring specialized hands-on skills in a highly variable and hazardous environment that AI cannot directly automate. The only minor AI exposure comes from digital documentation, report writing, and potential AI-assisted sonar/camera inspection analysis, but these are peripheral to the core occupation.
description: En anläggningsdykare utför fysiskt krävande undervattenarbeten som svetsning, skärning, bultning och inspektion av konstruktioner som broar, kajer, dammar, pipelines och fundament. De använder verktyg som hydrauliska skärare, undervattenssvetsningsutrustning, borrmaskiner och pneumatiska verktyg, samt kommunikationsutrustning för kontakt med ytan. Arbetet är nästan uteslutande fysiskt och manuellt – digitala inslag begränsas till eventuell dokumentation, rapportskrivning och användning av sonarutrustning eller undervatttenskameror vid inspektioner.

name: Isoleringsmontörer
rationale: Insulation workers perform highly physical, hands-on tasks — measuring, cutting, and fitting insulation materials in varied physical environments like construction sites and industrial spaces — which AI cannot automate directly. The only minor AI exposure comes from digitizing blueprints/work orders or AI-assisted planning tools, but the core manual installation work remains fully human-dependent.
description: **Isoleringsmontörer** arbetar med att montera och installera isolering på rör, ventilationskanaler, industriell utrustning och byggnader för att bevara värme, kyla eller skydda mot brand och fukt. Det dagliga arbetet är utpräglat fysiskt och innebär att mäta, skära och fästa isoleringsmaterial som mineralull, cellplast eller skumgummi med hjälp av verktyg som knivar, saxar, måttband, lim och mekaniska fästdon. Arbetet utförs på byggarbetsplatser, i industrimiljöer eller tekniska utrymmen och är i princip helt manuellt – digitala inslag begränsas i så fall till att läsa ritningar eller arbetsordrar på en surfplatta.

name: Glastekniker
rationale: Glaziers perform highly physical, hands-on work involving cutting, fitting, and installing glass on-site, which cannot be automated by AI in any near-term meaningful way. While AI could marginally assist with estimating, scheduling, or reading technical drawings, the core manual craft requires physical presence, dexterity, and real-world problem-solving that places it firmly in the low-exposure range.
description: A Swedish Glastekniker performs primarily **physical, hands-on work** — measuring, cutting, and installing glass in buildings, vehicles, and other structures, including windows, glass facades, mirrors, and safety glass. They use tools such as glass cutters, suction cups, glazing knives, sealant guns, and measuring equipment to fit and secure glass panels with putty, rubber profiles, or adhesives. Some tasks involve reading technical drawings and increasingly working with insulated glass units (IGU) or energy-efficient glazing systems, but the core of the job remains manual and on-site.

name: Målare
rationale: Painters perform highly physical, hands-on work applying coatings to surfaces that requires manual dexterity, spatial judgment, and physical presence on-site — none of which AI can replicate directly. While AI might marginally assist with scheduling, color selection tools, or estimating, the core craft work remains almost entirely resistant to automation.
description: En målare utför fysiskt arbete med att måla och behandla ytor på byggnader, både invändigt och utvändigt – de stryker på färg, lack, puts eller andra ytbeläggningar med penslar, rollers, sprututrustning och spackelverktyg. Innan målning förbereder de underlaget genom att slipa, sparka, grundmåla och skydda omkringliggande ytor med tejp och skyddspapper. Arbetet är helt hantverksmässigt och fysiskt till sin natur, och digitala verktyg spelar en mycket liten roll i det dagliga arbetet.

name: Skorstensfejare
rationale: Chimney sweeping is fundamentally a physical, on-site occupation requiring manual dexterity, specialized tools, and direct inspection of infrastructure that AI cannot remotely replicate. The administrative reporting component has minor AI exposure, but the overwhelming core of the work — climbing, cleaning, and physically inspecting chimneys — creates a strong barrier to automation.
description: A Swedish skorstensfejare performs primarily **physical, hands-on work**, inspecting and cleaning chimneys, flues, ventilation systems, and fireplaces in residential and commercial buildings to prevent fire hazards and ensure proper draft. They use tools such as rotating brushes, vacuum equipment, inspection cameras, and measuring instruments to remove soot and creosote deposits and check for structural damage or blockages. Beyond cleaning, they also carry out **mandatory fire safety inspections (sotning och brandskyddskontroll)** on behalf of the municipality, documenting findings and issuing reports — which involves some digital administration but the core work remains firmly physical and on-site.

name: Stålkonstruktionsmontörer och grovplåtsslagare
name_en: Steel structure erectors and heavy plate workers
rationale: Steel structure erectors and heavy plate workers perform highly physical, hands-on tasks involving cranes, welding, and heavy lifting on construction sites and industrial facilities, making direct AI automation extremely difficult. While AI may assist with planning, design, or quality control tools, the core work requires physical presence, manual dexterity, and real-time judgment in variable environments that are deeply resistant to AI substitution.
description: **Stålkonstruktionsmontörer och grovplåtsslagare** arbetar med att montera, rikta och fästa tunga stålkonstruktioner som broar, hallbyggnader, cisterner och industrianläggningar, samt tillverkar och formar grovplåt till behållare, rör och liknande strukturer. De använder verktyg och utrustning som kranar, lyftredskap, svetsaggregat, skärbågar, bultpistoler, vinkelslipar och mätinstrument för att säkerställa att konstruktionerna monteras enligt ritningar och toleranskrav. Arbetet är till övervägande del tungt fysiskt och utförs på byggarbetsplatser, verkstadsgolv eller industrianläggningar – digitala in

name: Smeder
name_en: Blacksmiths, hammersmiths and forging press workers
rationale: Blacksmithing and forging work is fundamentally physical, requiring direct manipulation of hot metal using hammers, anvils, presses, and rollers in industrial or craft forge settings — tasks AI cannot perform. While CNC and digital control systems assist with press and furnace operations, the core hands-on metalworking skill and physical presence requirement creates a strong natural barrier to AI displacement.
description: Swedish smeder arbetar primärt med fysiskt hantverk där de hettar upp metall i smedseldstäder eller ugnar och sedan formar den genom hamrande, pressning eller valsning med verktyg som hammare, städ, smidespressar och valsverk. Deras dagliga arbete innebär att tillverka eller reparera metalldetaljer, verktyg och konstruktionsdelar – antingen genom traditionellt handsmiде eller industriell smidning med hydrauliska eller mekaniska pressar. Arbetet är i grunden fysiskt och manuellt, även om moderna smedjer kan använda digitala styrsystem (CNC) för pressar och ugnar, men kärnarbetet sker alltid direkt med het metall och kraftfulla maskiner.
